description:
Tria Rink, located at 180 Kellogg Blvd W in downtown St. Paul, Minnesota, is the official practice facility of the Minnesota Wild, the state's NHL professional hockey team. This premium ice skating facility sits adjacent to the Xcel Energy Center and provides public ice skating opportunities in one of the most prestigious ice facilities in the Upper Midwest. Tria Rink features a single NHL-regulation sheet of ice with professional-grade conditions and amenities. The facility's affiliation with the Minnesota Wild ensures that public skaters experience championship-level ice quality, creating an exceptional ice skating environment for recreational skaters and ice skating enthusiasts throughout the Twin Cities.

Tria Rink offers diverse programming including public skating sessions for recreational skaters of all ages, figure skating programming, and adult hockey opportunities. The facility operates on a schedule that accommodates both Minnesota Wild practice and training needs as well as public ice skating availability. Public skating sessions welcome families with young children, beginner skaters taking their first steps on ice, and experienced recreational skaters seeking quality ice time. Figure skating clubs and coaching are available for skaters pursuing competitive figure skating or recreational improvement. Adult hockey leagues provide competitive opportunities for recreational hockey players throughout the St. Paul and Twin Cities region. The downtown St. Paul location near Xcel Energy Center makes Tria Rink exceptionally convenient for visitors to the Minnesota Wild and the vibrant St. Paul sports district.

Visiting Tria Rink offers access to an elite-level ice skating facility affiliated with professional hockey excellence. The downtown St. Paul location provides convenient parking and close proximity to restaurants, bars, and entertainment options in the Kellogg Blvd district. Public skating sessions must be confirmed in advance, as the schedule accommodates Minnesota Wild professional activities. Modern amenities include skate rentals, concessions, comfortable spectator seating, and downtown accessibility. Whether you want to skate at the same facility where NHL professionals train, experience championship ice conditions, or simply enjoy quality recreational ice skating in downtown St. Paul, Tria Rink delivers an unforgettable experience. Visit triarink.com for current public skating schedules and program information.
what_to_know: Check triarink.com for public skating schedules, as times vary based on Minnesota Wild practice and professional activities
Public skating availability is seasonal and subject to professional hockey operations; call ahead to confirm times before your visit
Skate rentals are available; arrive 15-20 minutes early for proper fitting and size selection
Figure skating coaching and programming are available; contact the facility for lesson options and club information
Adult hockey leagues operate at the facility; inquire about league divisions, skill levels, and registration requirements
Downtown St. Paul location provides convenient parking options and easy access to restaurants and entertainment
The facility's affiliation with the Minnesota Wild means you're skating where NHL professionals train; it's a unique Twin Cities experience

faq.question: When is public skating available at Tria Rink in St. Paul?
faq.answer: Public skating schedules vary and are subject to Minnesota Wild practice and professional activities. Visit triarink.com or call the facility to confirm current public skating times before planning your visit.

faq.question: Are skate rentals available at Tria Rink?
faq.answer: Yes, skate rentals are available. Arrive 15 minutes early to allow time for proper fitting and size selection.

faq.question: Does Tria Rink offer figure skating lessons and coaching?
faq.answer: Yes, figure skating programming and coaching are available. Contact the facility to learn about available coaches, lesson scheduling, and figure skating program details.

faq.question: Can adults play hockey at Tria Rink?
faq.answer: Yes, adult hockey leagues operate at the facility. Call to inquire about league divisions, skill levels, team opportunities, and registration information.

faq.question: Can I watch the Minnesota Wild practice at Tria Rink?
faq.answer: Tria Rink is the official Minnesota Wild practice facility. Contact the facility or check the Wild's official website for information about open practice sessions and spectator opportunities.

description:
Aldrich Arena, located at 1850 White Bear Ave N in Maplewood, Minnesota, is a Ramsey County-operated ice skating facility serving the east Twin Cities metropolitan area including Maplewood, North St. Paul, Oakdale, and surrounding communities. This community-focused ice arena features two full-sized sheets of ice dedicated to affordable public skating, recreational figure skating, and youth hockey programming. Aldrich Arena is known as one of the Twin Cities' most accessible community rinks, prioritizing affordability and family-friendly programming. The facility's commitment to community ice skating access makes it a popular choice for families seeking quality ice time at reasonable rates throughout the Ramsey County region.

The arena offers comprehensive ice skating programming including public skating sessions for recreational skaters of all ages, professional figure skating instruction and club opportunities, and youth hockey leagues serving multiple age groups and skill levels. Aldrich Arena's two-sheet configuration allows simultaneous programming, ensuring regular public skating availability throughout the week. The facility is particularly known for strong learn-to-skate programs that help beginner skaters build fundamental skills and confidence on ice. Youth hockey programming at Aldrich Arena serves the growing hockey community in Maplewood, North St. Paul, and Oakdale, with competitive leagues supporting players from elementary through high school levels. Figure skating clubs provide opportunities for recreational and competitive figure skaters throughout the Ramsey County area.

Visiting Aldrich Arena provides access to quality ice skating with exceptional value through its city-operated pricing structure. The Maplewood location on White Bear Ave N is convenient for residents of Maplewood, North St. Paul, Oakdale, and the broader east metro area. The facility's commitment to affordability combined with two sheets of quality ice makes it an excellent choice for families and recreational skaters seeking regular ice time. Modern amenities include skate rentals, concessions, comfortable spectator seating, and ample parking. Aldrich Arena's reputation for welcoming staff and consistent ice quality ensures a positive experience for all visitors, regardless of skating experience level.

description:
Parade Ice Garden, located at 600 Kenwood Pkwy in Minneapolis, Minnesota, is a historic Minneapolis Parks and Recreation facility representing decades of community ice skating tradition. This two-sheet ice arena sits in a beautiful location near downtown Minneapolis and the scenic Chain of Lakes area, providing ice skating access to diverse Minneapolis neighborhoods. Parade Ice Garden is a historically significant ice skating venue in the Twin Cities region, serving generations of Minneapolis families and skaters. The facility's convenient location near I-394 and downtown Minneapolis makes it easily accessible for the broader Twin Cities metropolitan area. The facility offers quality public skating, figure skating, and hockey programming with emphasis on community access and affordability through Minneapolis Parks and Recreation pricing.

The arena offers comprehensive ice skating programming including public skating sessions for recreational skaters of all ages, figure skating instruction and club opportunities, and youth and adult hockey leagues. The two-sheet configuration at Parade Ice Garden allows simultaneous programming, ensuring consistent public skating availability throughout the week. Figure skating clubs operate programs serving recreational and competitive skaters throughout Minneapolis and the Twin Cities. Youth hockey leagues serve the growing hockey community in Minneapolis, with programs supporting players from beginner to competitive levels. Public skating sessions welcome families with young children, beginner skaters, and experienced recreational skaters seeking quality ice time at reasonable public rates. The facility's historic atmosphere combined with excellent ice conditions creates a unique Minneapolis ice skating experience.

Visiting Parade Ice Garden connects you to historic Minneapolis ice skating tradition while enjoying quality facilities and convenient location. The Kenwood Pkwy location near the Chain of Lakes provides beautiful surroundings and close proximity to Minneapolis restaurants, shops, and entertainment options. The facility's commitment to community ice skating access through Minneapolis Parks and Recreation pricing makes it an excellent value for families throughout the Twin Cities. Modern amenities include skate rentals, concessions, spectator seating, and convenient parking near downtown Minneapolis. Whether you're seeking to experience historic ice skating tradition, enjoy quality recreational skating near downtown Minneapolis, or participate in organized leagues, Parade Ice Garden offers programming and atmosphere worth visiting.

description:
Braemar Arena, located at 7501 Ikola Way in Edina, Minnesota, is one of the Twin Cities' premier multi-sheet ice skating facilities featuring three full-sized sheets of ice. This world-class ice arena serves the Edina area and southwest Twin Cities metropolitan region including Eden Prairie and surrounding communities. Braemar Arena is home to Edina high school hockey programs, which represent one of Minnesota's most storied and competitive hockey traditions. The facility attracts serious ice skaters and hockey enthusiasts from throughout the Twin Cities seeking championship-level ice quality and professional programming. The three-sheet configuration at Braemar Arena ensures excellent availability for public skating, figure skating, hockey leagues, and special events simultaneously. The facility is a hub for the vibrant figure skating community throughout the southwest metro area.

Braemar Arena offers comprehensive ice skating programming including quality public skating sessions for recreational skaters of all ages, professional figure skating instruction and competitive club programs, youth hockey leagues serving multiple age groups, and adult hockey opportunities. The facility's three sheets of ice allow diverse programming to operate simultaneously, making Braemar Arena one of the most accessible premier facilities in the Twin Cities. Figure skating is particularly strong at Braemar, with experienced coaches and competitive opportunities attracting serious figure skaters throughout southwest Minnesota. The Edina high school hockey program's home venue status means Braemar Arena features some of the most competitive youth hockey in Minnesota. Public skating sessions welcome families with young children, beginner skaters, and experienced recreational skaters. The facility's professional ice conditions and comprehensive amenities create an exceptional ice skating experience.

Visiting Braemar Arena provides access to one of the Twin Cities' most prestigious ice skating facilities with exceptional three-sheet configuration. The Edina location on Ikola Way serves the southwest metro communities of Edina, Eden Prairie, and surrounding areas with convenient access and ample parking. Braemar Arena's reputation for competitive excellence combined with welcoming public skating programs makes it an ideal destination for ice skaters of all levels. Modern amenities include skate rentals, concessions, comfortable spectator seating, and professional coaching availability. Whether you want to experience championship-level ice conditions, watch some of Minnesota's best high school hockey, or participate in figure skating excellence, Braemar Arena represents the premier ice skating destination in southwest Twin Cities. Visit edinamn.gov/braemar for complete schedule and program information.
